title: Kedareshvara Temple, Balligavi
tagline: Kedareshvara Temple, Balligavi — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Kedareshvara temple (also spelt Kedareshwara or Kedaresvara) is located in the town of Balligavi (known variously in ancient inscriptions as Belagami, Belligave, Ballagamve and
overview.description: The Kedareshvara temple (also spelt Kedareshwara or Kedaresvara) is located in the town of Balligavi (known variously in ancient inscriptions as Belagami, Belligave, Ballagamve and Ballipura), near Shikaripura in the Shimoga district of Karnataka state, India. Dotted with centres of learning (agrahara), Balligavi was an important city during the 11th - 12th century Western Chalukya rule. The term Anadi Rajadhani (ancient capital) used in medieval inscriptions to describe this town tells a tale of great antiquity. topPlaces.name: Balligavi
topPlaces.description: Balligavi a town in Shikaripura taluk Shivamogga district of Karnataka state, India, is today known as Belagami or Balagame. Its ancient names are Baligrama, Dakshina Kendra, Valliggame and Valligrame.

topPlaces.name: Tripurantaka Temple
topPlaces.description: The Tripurantaka Temple (also called Tripurantakesvara or Tripurantakeshwara) was built around c. 1070 CE by the Western Chalukyas.

topPlaces.name: Shiralakoppa
topPlaces.description: Shiralakoppa is a panchayat town in Shikaripur Taluk, Shivamogga district in the Indian state of Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Talagunda
topPlaces.description: Talagunda is a village in the Shikaripura taluk of Shivamogga district in the state of Karnataka, India. Many inscriptions found here have provided insights into the rise of the Kadamba Dynasty.

topPlaces.name: Talagunda pillar inscription
topPlaces.description: The Tālagunda pillar inscription of Kakusthavarman is an epigraphic record in Sanskrit found in the ruined Pranavalingeshwara temple northwest of village Talagunda, Karnataka, India. It is engraved on hard grey granite and dated to between 455 and 470 CE. It gives an account of a Kadamba dynasty and the times of king Śāntivarma in northwest Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Adaganti
topPlaces.description: Adaganti is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Shikarpur taluk of Shimoga district in Karnataka.

items.note: Balligavi a town in Shikaripura taluk Shivamogga district of Karnataka state, India, is today known as Belagami or Balagame. Its ancient names are Baligrama, Dakshina Kendra, Valliggame and Valligrame.
The Tripurantaka Temple (also called Tripurantakesvara or Tripurantakeshwara) was built around c. 1070 CE by the Western Chalukyas.

Talagunda is a village in the Shikaripura taluk of Shivamogga district in the state of Karnataka, India. Many inscriptions found here have provided insights into the rise of the Kadamba Dynasty.

The Tālagunda pillar inscription of Kakusthavarman is an epigraphic record in Sanskrit found in the ruined Pranavalingeshwara temple northwest of village Talagunda, Karnataka, India. It is engraved on hard grey granite and dated to between 455 and 470 CE. It gives an account of a Kadamba dynasty and the times of king Śāntivarma in northwest Karnataka.
Adaganti is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Shikarpur taluk of Shimoga district in Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kedareshvara Temple, Balligavi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kedareshvara Temple, Balligavi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kedareshvara Temple, Balligavi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Hubli Airport (109 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (106 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kedareshvara Temple, Balligavi?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kedareshvara Temple, Balligavi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kedareshvara Temple, Balligavi?
faq.a: Stays in Kedareshvara Temple, Balligavi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kedareshvara Temple, Balligavi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Balligavi, Tripurantaka Temple, Shiralakoppa, Talagunda, Talagunda pillar inscription.
